name: Balsamiq Wireframing API
what_it_does: Low-fidelity wireframing tool with REST API — enabling UX designers and product teams to create quick, hand-drawn style wireframes and mockups for early-stage product design, with collaboration features, Atlassian (Jira/Confluence) integration, and Balsamiq Cloud REST API for project and wireframe management.
use_cases: Managing Balsamiq Cloud projects and wireframes programmatically via REST API
Exporting wireframes as images or PDF via Balsamiq API for documentation and handoff
Integrating Balsamiq wireframes with Jira issues for product requirement linking
Querying project and wireframe metadata for design system tracking
Automating Balsamiq project creation for new product features via API
not_for: High-fidelity interactive prototypes (use Figma or InVision for pixel-perfect design)
Full design system management (use Figma for component libraries and design tokens)
Development-ready handoff (use Zeplin or Figma for developer handoff with specs)
best_when: A product or UX team needs rapid, low-fidelity wireframing at the earliest design stages — communicating concepts without getting distracted by visual details — especially teams using Jira/Confluence who want native Atlassian integration.
avoid_when: You need high-fidelity prototypes, interactive animations, developer specs/handoff, or are past the early ideation stage.

auth.notes: API key from Balsamiq Cloud account settings. HTTP Basic auth with email as username and API key as password. Account-scoped access. No webhooks. No OAuth2. Atlassian Jira and Confluence plugins available separately. Balsamiq for Desktop is separate from Cloud — no API for Desktop version.

pricing.notes: Italian company (Bologna). Founded 2008. Bootstrapped and profitable. Intentionally simple — founder Peldi Guilizzoni famous for focusing on simplicity. Low-fidelity philosophy: handwritten style removes design distraction. 700,000+ users globally. Atlassian Marketplace integration. Annual revenue ~$10M (estimated). B-Corp certified. Used extensively in agile product development for sprint planning and user story creation.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for projects and wireframes. HTTP Basic auth with email + API key. Simple, well-documented API for a focused tool. No webhooks. Desktop version has no API — Cloud only. Limited API scope (project and wireframe management). Export to PNG/PDF via API. Atlassian plugins are separate from the Cloud API.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Project reads fully idempotent. Wireframe reads idempotent. Export reads idempotent (same content, same output).
known_agent_gotchas: HTTP Basic auth with email + API key as password (not standard Bearer token)
Desktop version has no API — Cloud only; check which version customers use
No webhooks — must poll for project/wireframe changes
API scope limited — wireframe content creation requires Balsamiq UI
Free tier limited to 2 projects — insufficient for team-level design operations

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. US data residency. HTTP Basic auth — weaker than OAuth2; use API key rotation. Italian company (Bologna) with GDPR compliance. Wireframe data may contain early-stage product concepts — treat as confidential IP. B-Corp certified — privacy practices are part of corporate values.
